title: The One With The Halloween Party

lines.text: Central Perk, everyone is there as Chandler and Monica enter. Oh, and Joey is wearing a FDNY T-shirt to make this the first nod to the tragedy that Friends have made.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey you guys?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know it's last minute, but we decided to have a Halloween party.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good! (And there's general excitement.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And everybody has to wear costumes. (And there's general disconcertment.) Come on! It'll be fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'll-I'll be there. I mean I have to wear a costume to all my classes that day anyway so...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please tell me you're not gonna dress up like a dinosaur.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (exhales sarcastically) Not two years in a row.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I'll come to the party but I'm not dressing up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You have to!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No way! Look, Halloween is so stupid! Dressing up, pretending to be someone you're not...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're an actor!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Ross, are you gonna bring Mona?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I think I will.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That hot girl from their wedding?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well hey-hey if she needs any idea for costumes, she could be a bikini model, or a slutty nurse, or a sexy cheerleader huh-Ooh-ooh, Leatherface from The Texas Chainsaw Massacre-No-no-no! Slutty Leatherface.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now wasn't Joey hitting on her at the wedding too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's right! He was hitting on her, and I got her. I guess the better man won. (To Joey) Please don't take her from me.
Opening Credits
A Street, Phoebe is walking down it and passes Ursula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ursula! (Ursula turns, smiles, and continues walking.) Wait! Err-err, it's me! Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Oh, I thought there was a mirror there. Okay, bye-bye. (Starts to walk again.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait a second! So, what's new with you?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Umm, nothing. I mean, I'm getting married next week.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah, it's gonna be a small ceremony. Just family. (Phoebe looks at her.) His.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh. Okay. Well, I'm really happy for you. (Starts to walk away.)

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Wait! If umm, if you want to come, I guess that'd be okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Sure! Why not? You could be my sister for the day.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Okay. Umm, y'know, my friends are having a Halloween party tonight at my old apartment so, you could come. Maybe I could meet the guy you're marrying.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Huh. Well, I'm supposed to be working at the restaurant tonight. I'm supposed to be working right now, so who cares.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: By the way, it's a costume party.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Oh! Okay, so that's why you're... (Motions to what she's wearing.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looks down) No. But thanks. (Walks away.)
Monica and Chandler's, The Halloween party has started. Monica is setting out some food as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait! You're supposed to wear a costume!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am! I am a woman who spent a lot of money on a dress and she wants to wear it, because soon she won't be able to fit into it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm Catwoman, who wants to borrow the dress when you're too big for it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Kids
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh! Can I give out the candy? I really want to be with the kids right now. Y'know, ever since I got pregnant I-I have the strongest maternal instincts.

lines.speaker: Kids
lines.text: Trick or treat!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to them) Just a minute!!! (She takes the candy and opens the door to two parents, a witch, a clown, and a cowgirl.) Look at you guys! Wow! You are a very scary witch. (Gives her candy.)

lines.speaker: Witch
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you are a very funny clown. (Gives him candy.)

lines.speaker: Clown
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to the cowgirl) And you are so in style right now. Y'know, I work at Ralph Lauren and the whole fall line has got this like equestrian theme going on. I don't suppose you saw the cover of British Vogue, but...

lines.speaker: Cowgirl
lines.text: (interrupting) Can I just have the candy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Sure. (Throws some in her bag and she walks away as Phoebe, dressed as Supergirl walks up and eyes Monica who eyes her back.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah, Catwoman. So we meet again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So we do Supergirl.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it's me. Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering from the bedroom wearing a big, pink bunny costume) Monica! Can I talk to you for a second? Listen, I appreciate you getting me the costume...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Monica) Oh, you did this to him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! I thought he'd love it! His favorite kid's book was the Velveteen Rabbit!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Velveteen Rabbit was brown and white!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it was either a pink bunny or no bunny at all.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No bunny at all!! Always no bunny at all!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't dress up either?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes I did! I'm Chandler. (Looks at Chandler) Dude, what happened?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How is that me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. I'm Chandler (makes a growling/gurgling sound at the end and the girls laugh.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Chandler) That is so you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When have I ever done that?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When have I ever done that?! (And does the sound again.)
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! (Opens the door to reveal a ballerina) Well you're just the prettiest ballerina I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: Thank you. (Pirouettes.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow! That deserves another piece of candy.

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: Thank you. (Does another ballerina move.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I have to say that earns tutu pieces of candy.

lines.speaker: Ballerina
lines.text: I love you! (Hugs Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh... Oh, honey here. Take it all. (Pours the entire large bowl into her bag and closes the door.) Monica! We need more candy?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! There's only been like four kids.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know, but one of them just said that she loved me so I just gave her everything.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No wonder your pregnant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey! (He's wearing a costume as well.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you supposed to be?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Remember the Russian satellite, Sputnik? (They all look at him.) Well, I'm a potato or a...spud. And these are my antennae. (Points to the colander with an old TV antenna glued on top that he's wearing.) So Sputnik, becomes... (They're still confused) Spud-nik. Spudnik!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! I don't have the worst costume anymore!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sees Ross) Hey all right, Ross came as doody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I-I'm not doody.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, space doody!

Joey gives him the okay symbol, and Ross rushes towards him to be stopped by Chandler. Meanwhile, Phoebe goes over to the snack table as some guy, which turns out to be Ursula's fiancee Eric, walks in and smacks her butt.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Aren't you gonna give me a kiss?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I will. But right after you tell me who the hell you are.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Ursula?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ursula's fiancee?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh my God, you're the sister!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Okay, I just slapped my future sister-in-law's ass.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I'm an idiot. Uh, is your mother here? Maybe I can give her a little slap on the butt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My mother killed herself.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She, now I knew that and...now I'm sweating. Look at me, I'm really sweating-Now I'm saying, "Look at me," I'm getting even sweatier. I think I probably should go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no! That's okay, we'll just start over. Okay? Hi! I'm Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Eric. (They shake hands and he's squinting. And, no, it's not me.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why are you looking at me like that?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: 'Cause the sweat's getting in my eyes and its burning.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Hands him a napkin.) So, what are you?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I don't think they have a name for it. It's just I get nervous; I start sweating like crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (laughs) No I-I meant your costume.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh umm, I'm the solar system. (He's wearing a black sweater with the planets glued on around the sun.) Yeah, my students helped me make it-I teach the second grade.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I love the second grade!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! It's so much better than first grade when you don't know what's going on and definitely better than third grade. Y'know with all the politics and mind games.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: So what do you do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I'm a masseuse...by day. (Stands with her hands on her hips like a Supergirl pose.)

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Y'know you don't have to stand here with me, believe me...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I'm having fun. I'm really-And I'm really-really excited for you and Ursula.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh I feel very lucky, she's great. I think she's the most beautiful woman I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.
[Time lapse, Monica is going over to talk to Joey.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You read comic books right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Exclusively.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who do you think would win in a fight, Catwoman or Supergirl?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Catwoman, hands down.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But between you and Phoebe, I'd have to give the edge to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Really?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you kiddin'? Phoebe lived on the street. Okay? Plus, she's got this crazy temper. She-She's not standing right behind me is she?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you're fine. (Joey checks anyway.) All right well, do you think I could take Rachel?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! Come on I am tough! Punch me right here! (Her stomach) As hard as you can!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you relax?! What are you taking this so seriously for? It doesn't matter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really? Okay? Well what would you say if I told you that, y'know, Ross or Chandler could beat you up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I would say, "Woman, please!"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (walking over) Hey. Ursula's fiancee is really sweet! He's a teacher, he does all this volunteer work. Y'know normally y'know, I don't like really sweaty guys. But this one? I could just mop him up!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're getting a crush on your sister's fiancee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No I'm not! You are!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Monica) Here comes the temper.
There's a knock on the door and Rachel opens it to a little girl.

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Y'know what honey, we're actually out of candy right now. But someone just went out to get some and I have been giving out money but I'm out of that too. Hey, can I write you a check?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, what's your name?

lines.speaker: Girl
lines.text: Lelani Mayolanofavich.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I'm just gonna write this out to cash.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Mona!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Hi!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey's gonna be thrilled! He was hoping you'd come by as a slutty nurse.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Umm, actually I'm just a nurse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You'd think that would embarrass me, but you see I'm maxed out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You made it!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Wait-wait! You're umm, you're a potato...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I'm a spud...

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: And the antennae...Oh my God you're Spudnik!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Marry her.
[Cut to Joey and Monica.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here's a good one for ya. Who do think would win in a fight between Ross and Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't answer that! Chandler's my husband.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So Ross?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.
[Cut to Phoebe and Eric.]

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hey beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello handsome. (Sees Ursula over her shoulder) Oh God. (Walks away in shame as they kiss.) Oh look at you two. So when did you guys meet?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Two weeks ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Two weeks? That's it?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Yeah, I know it sounds crazy, and it's not like me to do something so impulsive, but she's just so perfect, and we have so much in common.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: We're both teachers.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh? (Ursula motions for Phoebe to keep quiet.)

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: And we were both in the Peace Corps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Peace Corps, really? (Ursula motions, "I don't know.")

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: In fact when we were building houses in Uruguay, we were, we were just two towns apart and we never met.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah. It wasn't a town when I got there, but it was a town when I left. (To Eric) Shall we get me really drunk?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Sure.
They walk away.
[Cut to Mona and Ross walking past Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Howdy doody.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's funny. Yeah. Y'know you're the funniest man here in a pink bunny costume his wife made him wear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh relax man, relax. You're looking a little flushed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Monica) Hey-hey-hey, I think we might find out the answer to our question.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What question?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Monica and I were talking about who could kick whose ass in a fight, you or Ross?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's no question.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you think Ross too?
Monica turns around slowly.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) You picked Ross?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross is really strong! Okay, he's the strongest out of all three of you! (Joey looks at her.) Except for Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I cannot believe you didn't pick me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, in her defense, she's right. I am stronger. I would destroy you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh really?! You think you're stronger? Why don't you prove it? (He pushes Ross who starts to fall backwards until Mona catches him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I'll prove it! I'll prove it like a theorem!!
They start to fight with Ross pulling on Chandler's ears and Chandler hitting Ross over the head with his carrot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait-wait!! Okay, stop it! Stop it! Stop! (Breaks it up.) Now listen, no one's gonna fight in this apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Monica! (Grabs her and pulls her into the living room.) People came to see a fight, let's give 'em what they came for!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hey, you guys could arm wrestle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Listen to the slutty nurse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) You're going down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah? You're going further down! Downtown!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Seriously guys, the trash talk is embarrassing.
Commercial Break
Monica and Chandler's, the party continues with Rachel leaning on the counter as Gunther walks in carrying candy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Gunther! You brought candy! Thank you so much for picking this up! You are so sweet.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, someday you are gonna make some man the luckiest guy in the world.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Kid
lines.text: Trick or treat!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Gotta go! (Opens the door to a boy in a cape.) Hi! Wow! There you go! (Hands him some candy.)

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: My friend Lewis told me you were giving out money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, we were but umm, now we've got candy.

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: I'd rather have the money.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that-that's not your choice. Happy Halloween!

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: This isn't fair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well is it fair that all you did was put on a cape and I gotta give you free stuff?

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: Shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You shut up!
The gang gets interested now.

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: You can't tell me to shut up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, I think I just did. And uh-oh, here it comes again. Shut up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Joey) Yeah I know-I'm good-I got it! (Joey slowly backs away.) (To the boy) Now wait a minute, I've got one more thing I have to say to you...oh right! Shut up!

lines.speaker: Boy in the Cape
lines.text: You're a mean old woman. (Runs away.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Wait no! Shut up-I mean don't cry! Let me get my checkbook! (Grabs her checkbook and runs after him.)
[Cut to Mona and Joey clearing the dining room table for the grudge match between Chandler and Ross.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Chandler) Look honey, you don't have to do this, okay? It's the strength you have inside that means the most to me. You're loyal, you're honest, and you have integrity! That's the kind of strength that I want in the man that I love!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That means nothing to me. (To Ross) Come on!
The Hallway, Phoebe is exiting Monica and Chandler's, and finds Ursula standing in the hallway smoking.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi liar!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know the only reason he's marrying you is because he thinks all the things you were saying about yourself were true.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Well they could be true.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But they're not!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, it's a fine line huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why are you lying to him?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: I don't know. He said he did all this stuff and then I said I did it too and he got so excited, it was really fun.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: (entering) Honey?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: (waving the cigarette in Phoebe's face) It's a filthy, disgusting habit and I want you to quit now!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She's helped so many people to quit smoking.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Y'know, we'd really better get going.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh right, you've got a church group meeting tonight.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right. (Walks down the stairs.)

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Well, it was nice meeting you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You too. And Ursula?! It was really nice meeting you tonight!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Pheebs come on! Bunny vs. Doody! We're waiting! (They go inside.) (To Chandler and Ross.) Okay. Okay guys, one match, winner take all. (They grasp each other's hand in preparation for battle.) Oh wait-wait! What does the winner get?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pride.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And dignity.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (laughing) Okay, if you say so. All right, ready? Set! Go!
They start wrestling, only they are unable to move either one's arm despite a huge strain on their faces and a cheering crowd.
[Time Lapse: the crowd has left and only Mona, Monica, Joey, and Phoebe are still watching to see who will be able to move the other's arm first. An event that has yet to happen.]

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (To Joey) Wow! They're both really strong.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or equally weak.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Oh God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler's making his sex face.
Basically Chandler's face looks like he's not all there and is staring off into the distance...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Chandler) So, you gettin' tired?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope! I can do this all day.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Me too. (Pause) Gettin' a little tired though.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God, I'm exhausted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look this is starting to look really bad for me. Okay? Mona, Mona's standing right over there. (Looks behind him.) Oh God, she's talking to Joey! You gotta let me win!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No way! If anything you've gotta let me win! My wife thinks I'm a wimp!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, at least you have a wife! I-I keep getting divorces and knockin' people up! And I'm dressed as doody.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're Spudnik.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, who are we kidding? I'm doody. Please? She's watchin'.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine. (He lets Ross win.) Oh no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (celebrating) Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (clapping) Yay! My hero!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to her) You're a weird lady.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: (entering) Hey. Ursula said she left her purse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: (finds it) What a relief. It has all the numbers of the people in her prayer chain.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure it does. Yeah, yeah.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Well, I guess I'll see you at the wedding. (Exits and Phoebe follows him into the hall.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm listen, I don't think...I don't think I'm gonna make it to the wedding. So I just want to wish you all the luck in the world.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I think we'll be okay. Besides it's so perfect and (whispering) she's been saving herself for me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay I can't let you do this! She's lying to you.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She is lying! And I bet I can prove it. Excuse me. (She grabs Ursula's purse and starts going through it and finds some papers.) Okay. Okay. Yeah-Not a prayer chain, but what looks like a detailed drawing of a bank floor plan. (Holds up her nametag.) Okay, here's the nametag from the restaurant where she works as a waitress! Not a teacher, a waitress. All right, here's her driver license, this oughta be good, she always lies about this. How old did she say she was?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: She told me she was 25.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I almost don't want to show this. (Hands it to him.) Just remember I'm a minute younger.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I am so stupid. Of course she was lying! She's not a teacher. There's not such a thing as the top secret elementary school for the children of spies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. You're not, you're not stupid.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I'm not smart. (Phoebe has no comeback.) I just wanted so much to...be impulsive once. To be romantic.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's good, you should be impulsive and you should be romantic. Just...you did it with the wrong person. (He looks at her.) What?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: It's just so weird, two people look so much alike, and so different.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: (yelling from downstairs) Eric!! Let's go!!

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I'd better go, deal...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, you should. (They shake hands.)

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hurry up I gotta pray!!
Phoebe hands Eric Ursula's purse and he walks away.
[Time Lapse: Rachel is returning from chasing down the boy in the cape.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Well, I had to give the kid fifty bucks to stop crying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not so bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I also had to go to a couple houses with him as his girlfriend. Oh, I am just awful with children!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on! You're good with kids. They're just crazy on Halloween. Y'know, they're all greedy and hopped up on sugar!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? You think that's all it is?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely! Halloween is the worst. Except for Christmas...and their birthdays. Kinda get a little crazy during the summer too. And anytime they're hungry or sleepy. Y'know, kids are tough. Good luck with that. (Walks away.)
Closing Credits
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Monica are standing in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look, I wanted to tell I'm-I'm sorry you lost.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, I've got a secret for ya. I let him win.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) Is that a secret or a lie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I let him win-Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Would you tell her I let you win please?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Yeah. (Sarcastically) Uh Chandler let me win. No, Chandler's really strong. Oh my arm is so sore. Oh nurse! (Waddles over to Mona.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am strong! I'll show you! (He sits down at the table.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler please!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh what's the matter? Are you scared?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Let's go big bunny!
They assume the starting position.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. 1...2...3-Go! (Once again he's at a stalemate, but this time he's in pain.) (Pause) I'm gonna kill myself!
End
